channel.title: Posts on VK
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on VK

item.title: On Finishing Things
item.description:
I have, at last count, eleven unfinished side projects. A CLI tool for managing dotfiles that got to 80% before I decided to rewrite it in a different language. A home automation dashboard that works perfectly except for the bits that don’t. A blog engine — not this one, a different one, also unfinished.

I am, it turns out, very good at starting things.

The 80% problem

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. item.title: Why Rust Is Hard (And Why That's the Point)
item.description:
The first time the Rust compiler rejected my code, I thought it was broken. The second time, I thought I was broken. By the hundredth time, I started to understand that the compiler was right all along — I just hadn’t been thinking carefully enough about ownership.

The borrow checker is a mirror

item.title: Kubernetes Is Not a Deploy Button
item.description:
There is a pattern I’ve seen at every company past a certain size. Someone reads a blog post, attends a KubeCon talk, or hears a competitor mention Kubernetes in a press release. A decision is made. Six months later, a team of four is maintaining a 47-node cluster to serve an application that previously ran fine on three EC2 instances.

This is not a Kubernetes problem. It’s a complexity budget problem.
